sections.biography-source-undefined:
Zhuge Jing was once asked by Sun Hao, “Sir, you are styled Zhongsi*, so what are you thinking that you want?” Zhuge Jing replied, “In home, I think about filial piety; in serving my liege, I think about loyalty; and with companions and friends, I think about trust. These are what I think about, and nothing more.”

*The Si in Zhongsi means “to ponder”, so Sun Hao is making fun of Zhuge Jing’s name.

full_text:
Zhuge Jing (Zhongsi) 諸葛靚 (仲思)
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Zhuge Jing (諸葛靚), styledZhongsi (仲思), was born in Yangdu in Langya. Sent to Wu as a hostage, he became a prominent commander under Sun Hao. Zhuge Jing was affiliated with the Wu Kingdom. Zhuge Jing lived during the Three Kingdoms and Jin Dynasty period of Chinese history.
